_comment: Manifest read by installed copies of Whisper Dictate. Updated by release-public.ps1 as part of the dual-publish step. The 'version' field follows SemVer; the 'installer_url' must point at a downloadable .exe asset on a public release of this repo.
release_notes_md:
### Added

- **Focus-lost recovery toast.** When the engine detects that focus drifted away from the field that was active at record-time (user clicked elsewhere mid-dictation, an alert stole focus, etc.), a bottom-right amber toast appears: `⚠ Focus lost  ⧉ Copy transcription`. Clicking the button copies the transcription onto the clipboard so the user can paste it manually wherever they actually wanted. Auto-dismisses after 5 s. The paste itself still goes through (we attempt focus restore before sending Ctrl+V) — the toast is a recovery affordance for the case where the paste landed in the wrong place. New IPC: `dictate.py` writes a single-slot `last_paste_warning.json` (atomic tmp+replace) when it logs focus drift; `main_app.py` polls and surfaces the toast, deduping by timestamp high-water mark (initialized from the existing file at startup so cold-launch doesn't replay stale events).
- **Formatted "What's new" release notes.** The post-update modal now renders the CHANGELOG entry as proper markdown — headings, bullets, **bold**, `inline code`, fenced code blocks, links — instead of the v0.3.1 plain-text strip. New `_render_markdown_into_text()` helper in `main_app.py` parses the subset we use in CHANGELOG (h1/h2/h3, ordered/unordered lists, bold/italic, inline code, code blocks, links, horizontal rules) and inserts into a Tk `Text` widget with tag-based styling.

### Changed

- **`PasteWarningToast` uses a clickable `tk.Label` for the Copy action**, not `tk.Button` — same lesson as the v0.3.0 `UpdateToast`: buttons in an `overrideredirect` + `SWP_NOACTIVATE` Toplevel don't receive click events because the window isn't activate-able in Win32 terms. A Label with `<Button-1>` binding works reliably.

### Vocabulary

- New `WORD_MAP` entry: `VEVX → DevX` (covers `VEVX`, `VEVX,`, `VEVX.`, etc. + possessive).
